methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $2.7B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 11 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $600M - $5.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Xaira Therapeutics is a Seed-stage biotech company founded by Marc Tessier-Lavigne, currently valued at $2.7B post-money on $1B total raised. The valuation assessment draws from a comparable universe of 11 private peers in Biotech & Health at similar valuation tiers, including Abridge ($5.3B, Series E), Ultima Genomics ($4.0B, Series C), and Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B, Series B), alongside three public biotech comps (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L).

Two triangulation methods prove applicable. The Stage-Progression method applies a 2.5x Seed-to-next-stage multiplier to the $2.7B last post-money valuation, implying $6.8B. The Peer-Median method benchmarks against 11 same-sector peers, yielding a median valuation of $1.8B with a range spanning $600M to $5.3B. Public-Comp Anchoring cannot be deployed due to absence of revenue disclosure—standard for private companies. The median of the $1.8B peer result and the $6.8B stage-progression result is $4.3B.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $1.5B to $7.8B, with a median of $4.3B. This range reflects material divergence between peer-median positioning (lower quartile of comparable rounds) and stage-progression expectations (upper-end growth assumptions). Key uncertainties include the company's underlying revenue trajectory, IP quality relative to public benchmarks, and the appropriate step-up multiple given Seed-stage capital intensity in biotech.
